era_context: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI, Pepi II), Akkadian Empire (Naram-Sin), mature Harappan civilization. Continuation of the previous era. All three civilizations are stable but within a century of major transitions.

events.title: Pepi II's reign continues; Old Kingdom in late phase
events.description: Pepi II continues his reign. The Old Kingdom is in its final phase, with provincial governors gaining increasing power. The centralized state apparatus remains functional, but decentralization is accelerating. No major disruptions are recorded for this year.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for this year.

events.title: Naram-Sin's empire at its height
events.description: Naram-Sin continues to rule the Akkadian Empire at its territorial zenith. The empire controls vast territories and maintains administrative and military control. No major disruptions are recorded for this year.

sources.name: Royal inscriptions from the Akkadian period

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but specific events for individual years are rarely recorded.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is undifferentiated in the historical record. No specific events are recorded.
